question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user deliberately slow down their gRPC stream consumption to trigger reactor backpressure overflow on a high-volume topic, causing the DEADLINE_EXCEEDED error to terminate the stream and disrupt other legitimate subscribers on the same topic who are relying on the mirror node for smart contract event delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime set to a valid past timestamp and consensusEndTime set to a timestamp only 1 nanosecond after startTime, causing the filter to pass isValidEndTime() but produce a near-zero query window that causes the reactive stream to complete immediately, potentially causing smart contract event consumers to miss messages in that window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with both consensusStartTime and consensusEndTime set to timestamps that both convert to Long.MAX_VALUE via convertTimestamp(), causing isValidEndTime() (endTime > startTime) to fail since Long.MAX_VALUE is not > Long.MAX_VALUE, resulting in a ConstraintViolationException that reveals the internal timestamp clamping behavior?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a non-existent topic ID when grpcProperties.isCheckTopicExists() is false, causing the subscription to silently return an empty stream that never completes (no endTime set), holding open a gRPC stream indefinitely and consuming server resources without delivering any messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a valid topic with startTime=0 (full historical scan) and limit=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the reactive pipeline to attempt to stream the entire topic message history, overwhelming the network layer and causing unintended behavior for other consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isValidStartTime() in TopicMessageFilter uses DomainUtils.now() at filter build time (inside Mono.fromCallable), meaning a startTime set to exactly the current nanosecond could pass validation but represent a future time by the time the database query executes, causing the query to return no results and mislead smart contract event consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid topic IDs by probing subscribeTopic() with different topicID values and observing the difference between Status.NOT_FOUND (EntityNotFoundException) and Status.INVALID_ARGUMENT (not a valid topic), effectively mapping out which entity IDs exist and their types in the network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribeTopic() uses Mono.fromCallable which executes toFilter() synchronously on the gRPC thread, and by sending a large number of concurrent requests, cause thread contention in the gRPC thread pool that delays message delivery to legitimate smart contract event consumers?
